decisions.title_en: Musk v Altman jury selection day 1 in Oakland with 9-juror, two-phase trial structure revealed
decisions.reason: Previously published 04-23 (trial preview) and 04-25 (Musk drops fraud claims). Today: trial actually begins with jury selection. New procedural facts: 9 jurors no alternates, court Mon-Thu 8:30am-1:40pm, two-phase liability+remedies structure, jury verdict is advisory-only, expected through mid-May, each side gets ~20 hrs of trial time, Microsoft gets 5 hrs. These are substantively new operational facts not in prior coverage.

decisions.title_en: US State Dept dispatches global diplomatic cable warning about Chinese AI labs distilling US models
decisions.reason: story_key not in lookback. Cable dated April 24 instructs all diplomatic posts to raise concerns about DeepSeek/Moonshot/MiniMax extracting US AI models via distillation.

decisions.title_en: SpaceX IPO pitch reveals AI-first pivot, $75B raise at $1.75T, xAI absorbed posted $6.4B operating loss
decisions.reason: story_key not in lookback. Bloomberg analysis April 24: SpaceX confidentially filed June IPO, pitches itself to investors as AI company with $28.5T TAM. xAI unit (absorbed Feb 2026) had $6.4B operating loss in 2025 with $12.7B capex. Different from cursor-acquisition-option (04-21) and tesla-2b-ai-hardware-acquisition (04-23).

decisions.title_en: Canada's Cohere acquires Germany's Aleph Alpha to form sovereign AI giant; ~$20B Cohere valuation
decisions.reason: story_key not in lookback. Endorsed by both German + Canadian governments. Schwarz Group invests $600M in upcoming Cohere Series E. HQ Toronto + Berlin. Major sovereign-AI consolidation play.

decisions.title_en: Commerce Department fires Collin Burns from Center for AI Standards 4 days into job over Anthropic ties
decisions.reason: story_key not in lookback. New episode in Anthropic-WH tension series after anthropic-mythos-zerodays (04-19). Burns started Monday, was pushed out Thursday after WH hadn't been briefed; replaced by Chris Fall. Distinct from prior Mythos coverage — this is about a personnel decision, not Mythos itself.

decisions.title_en: OpenAI's Sora app/web actually shuts down; Disney $1B partnership ends
decisions.reason: story_key not in lookback. Distinct from openai-triple-exec-exit (04-17) which previewed the date but did not cover the actual execution or the Disney $1B deal cancellation. Today the consumer app/web is actually offline, with Disney's $1B partnership/character-licensing deal collapsing. API remains live until Sept 24.

decisions.title_en: Trump's December AI executive order deadlines all blown — FTC/DOJ/NTIA actions overdue
decisions.reason: story_key not in lookback. Three provisions due March 11 (FTC consumer-protection guidance, DOJ litigation task force, NTIA BEAD-funding policy notice) all past due more than a month. Raises doubts about state-AI-law preemption push.
Already published 04-24 (V4 release + Huawei Ascend deal) and 04-25 (further Huawei/Ascend confirmation). Weekend coverage is just rehash with no substantive new developments.

decisions.title_en: Anthropic publishes 'Claude is a space to think', commits ad-free
decisions.reason: Original post is February 4, 2026. April 21 medium articles are recap/republish, not new info. event_date 2026-02-04 is 82 days ago, far beyond 5-day limit.
